preamble.text: This document defines the authoritative mechanical rules of the game.
preamble.raw: - This document defines the authoritative mechanical rules of the game.

preamble.text: All systems, calculations, and resolutions described here are deterministic and take precedence over narrative interpretation unless explicitly stated otherwise.
preamble.raw: - All systems, calculations, and resolutions described here are deterministic and take precedence over narrative interpretation unless explicitly stated otherwise.

entries.text: This section defines all rules related to character advancement, experience expenditure, and power scaling.
entries.raw: - This section defines all rules related to character advancement, experience expenditure, and power scaling.

entries.text: All level progression, XP costs, Challenge Rating (CR), and Rank calculations are resolved exclusively using the rules in this section
entries.raw: - All level progression, XP costs, Challenge Rating (CR), and Rank calculations are resolved exclusively using the rules in this section

entries.text: Stats, Skills, and Supernatural Powers have Levels.
entries.raw: - Stats, Skills, and Supernatural Powers have Levels.

entries.text: Levels are increased by spending Experience Points (XP) using the XP Buy tables.
entries.raw: - Levels are increased by spending Experience Points (XP) using the XP Buy tables.

entries.text: Current cap: all Stats, Skills and Powers are limited to Level 5 unless explicitly stated otherwise.
entries.raw: - Current cap: all Stats, Skills and Powers are limited to Level 5 unless explicitly stated otherwise.

entries.text: XP Buy tables are listed below under their respective table names.
entries.raw: - XP Buy tables are listed below under their respective table names.

entries.text: CR and Rank are determined solely by XP Used.
entries.raw: - CR and Rank are determined solely by XP Used.

entries.text: XP ranges are inclusive on the lower bound and exclusive on the upper bound.
entries.raw: - XP ranges are inclusive on the lower bound and exclusive on the upper bound.

entries.text: XP Used is the total cumulative XP spent across all Stats, Skills, and Powers.
entries.raw: - XP Used is the total cumulative XP spent across all Stats, Skills, and Powers.

entries.text: XP Used determines the character's Challenge Rating (CR) and Rank.
entries.raw: - XP Used determines the character's Challenge Rating (CR) and Rank.

entries.text: Exception: the highest CR band has no upper XP bound.
entries.raw: - Exception: the highest CR band has no upper XP bound.

entries.text: This section defines the universal dice and success-resolution mechanics used by all systems unless explicitly stated otherwise.
entries.raw: - This section defines the universal dice and success-resolution mechanics used by all systems unless explicitly stated otherwise.

entries.text: This system uses a D10 success-counting mechanic.
entries.raw: - This system uses a D10 success-counting mechanic.

entries.text: The effective base difficulty threshold is 6.
entries.raw: - The effective base difficulty threshold is 6.

entries.text: Let N be the total dice pool size.
entries.raw: - Let N be the total dice pool size.

entries.text: N = relevant stat ranks + relevant skill ranks.
entries.raw: - N = relevant stat ranks + relevant skill ranks.

entries.text: Each die is evaluated independently.
entries.raw: - Each die is evaluated independently.

entries.text: Dice pool size (N) and die face value are separate concepts.
entries.raw: - Dice pool size (N) and die face value are separate concepts.

entries.text: Roll of 1: -1 success
entries.raw: - Roll of 1: -1 success

entries.text: Roll of 2-5: 0 successes
entries.raw: - Roll of 2-5: 0 successes

entries.text: Roll of 6-9: +1 success
entries.raw: - Roll of 6-9: +1 success

entries.text: Roll of 10:
entries.raw: - Roll of 10:

entries.text: +1 success if N < 10
entries.raw: - +1 success if N < 10

entries.text: +2 successes if N >= 10
entries.raw: - +2 successes if N >= 10

entries.text: Sum all success modifiers to obtain Total Successes.
entries.raw: - Sum all success modifiers to obtain Total Successes.

entries.text: If Total Successes < 0, the result is a Botch.
entries.raw: - If Total Successes < 0, the result is a Botch.

entries.text: A Botch represents complete failure.
entries.raw: - A Botch represents complete failure.

entries.text: Interpretation and consequences are determined by the DM.
entries.raw: - Interpretation and consequences are determined by the DM.

entries.text: No other botch conditions exist.
entries.raw: - No other botch conditions exist.

entries.text: This section defines the action types available each turn and the rules governing their use and conversion.
entries.raw: - This section defines the action types available each turn and the rules governing their use and conversion.

entries.text: Each character turn provides:
entries.raw: Each character turn provides:

entries.text: 1 Standard Action
entries.raw: - 1 Standard Action

entries.text: 1 Bonus Action
entries.raw: - 1 Bonus Action

entries.text: 1 Move Action
entries.raw: - 1 Move Action

entries.text: Reaction
entries.raw: - Reaction

entries.text: Used to perform a major action (attack, spell, complex interaction).
entries.raw: - Used to perform a major action (attack, spell, complex interaction).

entries.text: May be spent in place of one of the following:
entries.raw: - May be spent in place of one of the following:

children.text: a Move Action allowing up to 20 meters of movement, or
children.raw: - a Move Action allowing up to 20 meters of movement, or

children.text: Preparation of a Reaction (trigger defined at preparation time).
children.raw: - Preparation of a Reaction (trigger defined at preparation time).

entries.text: Represents minor actions (e.g., drawing or dropping a weapon, specific supernatural powers).
entries.raw: - Represents minor actions (e.g., drawing or dropping a weapon, specific supernatural powers).

entries.text: Cannot be converted into a Standard or Move Action.
entries.raw: - Cannot be converted into a Standard or Move Action.

entries.text: A Standard Action may be spent in place of a Bonus Action to gain an additional Bonus Action.
entries.raw: - A Standard Action may be spent in place of a Bonus Action to gain an additional Bonus Action.

entries.text: Allows movement up to 5 meters, unless modified by another rule.
entries.raw: - Allows movement up to 5 meters, unless modified by another rule.

entries.text: If a Standard Action is spent in place of a Move Action, the character may move up to 25 meters instead.
entries.raw: - If a Standard Action is spent in place of a Move Action, the character may move up to 25 meters instead.

entries.text: A Reaction is a contingent action that triggers automatically when a predefined condition occurs.
entries.raw: - A Reaction is a contingent action that triggers automatically when a predefined condition occurs.

entries.text: Reactions may be granted by rules, powers, or effects, or may be prepared in advance using a Standard Action.
entries.raw: - Reactions may be granted by rules, powers, or effects, or may be prepared in advance using a Standard Action.

entries.text: Only the specified trigger and response occur; no additional actions may be taken at that time.
entries.raw: - Only the specified trigger and response occur; no additional actions may be taken at that time.

entries.text: Unless explicitly stated otherwise, a Reaction may occur at most once per round.
entries.raw: - Unless explicitly stated otherwise, a Reaction may occur at most once per round.

entries.text: No other action conversions are allowed.
entries.raw: - No other action conversions are allowed.

entries.text: This section defines how physical and magical attacks are resolved, including precedence rules, defensive resolution, and damage calculation.
entries.raw: - This section defines how physical and magical attacks are resolved, including precedence rules, defensive resolution, and damage calculation.

entries.text: Every attack is either Physical or Magical.
entries.raw: - Every attack is either Physical or Magical.

entries.text: Physical attacks use Physical Attack Resolution below.
entries.raw: - Physical attacks use Physical Attack Resolution below.

entries.text: Magical attacks use Magical Attack Resolution below.
entries.raw: - Magical attacks use Magical Attack Resolution below.

entries.text: DR applies to Physical damage.
entries.raw: - DR applies to Physical damage.

entries.text: Soak applies to non-physical damage.
entries.raw: - Soak applies to non-physical damage.

entries.text: If any rule conflicts, this section overrides all others
entries.raw: - If any rule conflicts, this section overrides all others

entries.text: The recognized combat damage types are Fire, Cold, Acid, Lightning, Shadow, Radiant, Necrotic, Sonic, Mental, Divine, and Physical.
entries.raw: - The recognized combat damage types are Fire, Cold, Acid, Lightning, Shadow, Radiant, Necrotic, Sonic, Mental, Divine, and Physical.

entries.text: Fire, Cold, Acid, Lightning, and Sonic are Elemental damage types.
entries.raw: - Fire, Cold, Acid, Lightning, and Sonic are Elemental damage types.

entries.text: Resistance to each damage type is tracked separately using a resistance level from -2 to +2.
entries.raw: - Resistance to each damage type is tracked separately using a resistance level from -2 to +2.

entries.text: Resistance Level -2 = Fragile = x2 damage taken.
entries.raw: - Resistance Level -2 = Fragile = x2 damage taken.

entries.text: Resistance Level -1 = Vulnerable = x1.5 damage taken.
entries.raw: - Resistance Level -1 = Vulnerable = x1.5 damage taken.

entries.text: Resistance Level 0 = Normal = x1 damage taken.
entries.raw: - Resistance Level 0 = Normal = x1 damage taken.

entries.text: Resistance Level +1 = Resist = x0.5 damage taken.
entries.raw: - Resistance Level +1 = Resist = x0.5 damage taken.

entries.text: Resistance Level +2 = Immune = x0 damage taken.
entries.raw: - Resistance Level +2 = Immune = x0 damage taken.

entries.text: Unless explicitly stated otherwise, every combatant is Normal (0) to every damage type.
entries.raw: - Unless explicitly stated otherwise, every combatant is Normal (0) to every damage type.

entries.text: Effects that modify resistance change the tracked resistance level directly for the affected damage type.
entries.raw: - Effects that modify resistance change the tracked resistance level directly for the affected damage type.

entries.text: Attack delivery and damage mitigation are resolved separately.
entries.raw: - Attack delivery and damage mitigation are resolved separately.

entries.text: Physical damage is reduced by DR.
entries.raw: - Physical damage is reduced by DR.

entries.text: Non-physical damage is reduced by Soak, unless a rule explicitly states otherwise.
entries.raw: - Non-physical damage is reduced by Soak, unless a rule explicitly states otherwise.

entries.text: A Magical attack may still deal Physical damage; if so, it is reduced by DR instead of Soak.
entries.raw: - A Magical attack may still deal Physical damage; if so, it is reduced by DR instead of Soak.

entries.text: If a rule defines a different mitigation channel for a damage instance, that rule overrides the default mitigation channel.
entries.raw: - If a rule defines a different mitigation channel for a damage instance, that rule overrides the default mitigation channel.

entries.text: Magical attacks bypass Attack Roll and Dodge entirely.
entries.raw: - Magical attacks bypass Attack Roll and Dodge entirely.

entries.text: Magical attacks deal a fixed damage value as defined by the effect.
entries.raw: - Magical attacks deal a fixed damage value as defined by the effect.

entries.text: Final Magical Damage Taken = max(0, Magical Damage - applicable mitigation).
entries.raw: - Final Magical Damage Taken = max(0, Magical Damage - applicable mitigation).

entries.text: Applicable mitigation is determined by the mitigation channel rules in 4.1.3.
entries.raw: - Applicable mitigation is determined by the mitigation channel rules in 4.1.3.

entries.text: No dice are rolled during magical damage resolution unless explicitly stated by the effect.
entries.raw: - No dice are rolled during magical damage resolution unless explicitly stated by the effect.

entries.text: This section defines the step-by-step resolution of physical attacks, including attack rolls, defensive resolution, hit comparison, and damage calculation.
entries.raw: - This section defines the step-by-step resolution of physical attacks, including attack rolls, defensive resolution, hit comparison, and damage calculation.

entries.text: Determine attack dice pool:
entries.raw: - Determine attack dice pool:

entries.text: Melee: Dexterity + Melee + miscellaneous attack bonuses
entries.raw: - Melee: Dexterity + Melee + miscellaneous attack bonuses

entries.text: Ranged: Dexterity + Ranged + miscellaneous attack bonuses
entries.raw: - Ranged: Dexterity + Ranged + miscellaneous attack bonuses

entries.text: Roll using the D10 Success System.
entries.raw: - Roll using the D10 Success System.

entries.text: Let A be the total number of attack successes.
entries.raw: - Let A be the total number of attack successes.

entries.text: Dodge is the defensive mechanism; Armor Class (AC) is its numeric value.
entries.raw: - Dodge is the defensive mechanism; Armor Class (AC) is its numeric value.

entries.text: Dodge is a passive defense applied during hit resolution and does not consume actions or reactions.
entries.raw: - Dodge is a passive defense applied during hit resolution and does not consume actions or reactions.

entries.text: Dodge is ignored only if explicitly disabled by a rule (e.g., paralysis).
entries.raw: - Dodge is ignored only if explicitly disabled by a rule (e.g., paralysis).

entries.text: All bonuses that modify Dodge contribute to Armor Class (AC).
entries.raw: - All bonuses that modify Dodge contribute to Armor Class (AC).

entries.text: Defender Dodge value (Armor Class, AC): AC = Dexterity + miscellaneous dodge bonuses
entries.raw: - Defender Dodge value (Armor Class, AC): AC = Dexterity + miscellaneous dodge bonuses

entries.text: Compare A and AC:
entries.raw: - Compare A and AC:

entries.text: If AC > A, the attack misses.
entries.raw: - If AC > A, the attack misses.

entries.text: If A > AC, the attack hits and M = A - AC.
entries.raw: - If A > AC, the attack hits and M = A - AC.

entries.text: If A = AC:
entries.raw: - If A = AC:

entries.text: If a player character is involved, the player wins the tie.
entries.raw: - If a player character is involved, the player wins the tie.

entries.text: Otherwise, the defender wins.
entries.raw: - Otherwise, the defender wins.

entries.text: Determine attacker Damage Pool (DP):
entries.raw: - Determine attacker Damage Pool (DP):

entries.text: DP = weapon damage + all damage bonuses
entries.raw: - DP = weapon damage + all damage bonuses

entries.text: Total Damage Pool:
entries.raw: - Total Damage Pool:

entries.text: DP + M
entries.raw: - DP + M

entries.text: Roll the Total Damage Pool using the D10 Success System.
entries.raw: - Roll the Total Damage Pool using the D10 Success System.

entries.text: Final Damage Taken:
entries.raw: - Final Damage Taken:

entries.text: max(0, damage successes - defender DR)
entries.raw: - max(0, damage successes - defender DR)

entries.text: This section defines a character's core attributes and how each stat contributes to dice pools, defenses, and derived mechanics.
entries.raw: - This section defines a character's core attributes and how each stat contributes to dice pools, defenses, and derived mechanics.

entries.text: Stats represent a character's core physical, mental and social attributes.
entries.raw: - Stats represent a character's core physical, mental and social attributes.

entries.text: Stat levels are added directly to relevant dice pools when used.
entries.raw: - Stat levels are added directly to relevant dice pools when used.

entries.text: Unless explicitly stated, Stats do not grant effects outside the rules listed below.
entries.raw: - Unless explicitly stated, Stats do not grant effects outside the rules listed below.

entries.text: Adds to melee and brawl damage.
entries.raw: - Adds to melee and brawl damage.

entries.text: Determines carrying capacity.
entries.raw: - Determines carrying capacity.

entries.text: Excessive load may impose penalties at the DM's discretion.
entries.raw: - Excessive load may impose penalties at the DM's discretion.

entries.text: Used for physical actions such as breaking objects, forcing doors, and climbing.
entries.raw: - Used for physical actions such as breaking objects, forcing doors, and climbing.

entries.text: Determines melee, brawl, and ranged attack dice pools.
entries.raw: - Determines melee, brawl, and ranged attack dice pools.

entries.text: Determines base Dodge against physical attacks, including projectiles.
entries.raw: - Determines base Dodge against physical attacks, including projectiles.

entries.text: Used for initiative rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for initiative rolls.

entries.text: Initiative roll: DEX + WITS.
entries.raw: - Initiative roll: DEX + WITS.

entries.text: Determines base Hit Points (HP).
entries.raw: - Determines base Hit Points (HP).

entries.text: Base HP formula: HP = 2 + (STAM x 2).
entries.raw: - Base HP formula: HP = 2 + (STAM x 2).

entries.text: Determines base soak against supernatural (magical) damage.
entries.raw: - Determines base soak against supernatural (magical) damage.

entries.text: Magical damage may be reduced by Stamina soak.
entries.raw: - Magical damage may be reduced by Stamina soak.

entries.text: Used for convincing and persuasion rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for convincing and persuasion rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: CHA + Social.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: CHA + Social.

entries.text: Used for intimidation rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for intimidation rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: CHA + Intimidation.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: CHA + Intimidation.

entries.text: Used for seduction rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for seduction rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: APP + Social.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: APP + Social.

entries.text: Used for deception rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for deception rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: MAN + Social.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: MAN + Social.

entries.text: Deception includes lying, withholding information, and intentionally misleading responses.
entries.raw: - Deception includes lying, withholding information, and intentionally misleading responses.

entries.text: Used for knowledge-based rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for knowledge-based rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: INT + Social, unless a different skill is specified.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: INT + Social, unless a different skill is specified.

entries.text: Used for defensive rolls against convincing and persuasion attempts.
entries.raw: - Used for defensive rolls against convincing and persuasion attempts.

entries.text: Defensive dice pool: INT + Social.
entries.raw: - Defensive dice pool: INT + Social.

entries.text: Used for common sense and quick thinking rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for common sense and quick thinking rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: WITS + Social.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: WITS + Social.

entries.text: Used for defensive rolls against seduction attempts.
entries.raw: - Used for defensive rolls against seduction attempts.

entries.text: Defensive dice pool: WITS + Social.
entries.raw: - Defensive dice pool: WITS + Social.

entries.text: Used for self-control rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for self-control rolls.

entries.text: Self-control rolls use WITS alone, unless the DM specifies otherwise.
entries.raw: - Self-control rolls use WITS alone, unless the DM specifies otherwise.

entries.text: Used for initiative rolls.
entries.raw: - Used for initiative rolls.

entries.text: Initiative roll: DEX + WITS.
entries.raw: - Initiative roll: DEX + WITS.

entries.text: Used to locate traps and detect hidden entities.
entries.raw: - Used to locate traps and detect hidden entities.

entries.text: Dice pool: PER + Alertness.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: PER + Alertness.

entries.text: Used to oppose Stealth rolls.
entries.raw: - Used to oppose Stealth rolls.

entries.text: Dice pool: PER + Alertness.
entries.raw: - Dice pool: PER + Alertness.

entries.text: Used for defensive rolls against deception.
entries.raw: - Used for defensive rolls against deception.

entries.text: Defensive dice pool: PER + Social.
entries.raw: - Defensive dice pool: PER + Social.

entries.text: Contributes to ranged attack dice pools.
entries.raw: - Contributes to ranged attack dice pools.

entries.text: Ranged bonus dice: floor((PER - 1) / 2) d10.
entries.raw: - Ranged bonus dice: floor((PER - 1) / 2) d10.

entries.text: This section defines all skill- and power-based mechanics, including dice pool contributions, special effects, scaling rules, and resource interactions.
entries.raw: - This section defines all skill- and power-based mechanics, including dice pool contributions, special effects, scaling rules, and resource interactions.

entries.text: The level of a T1 Skill is always added to the relevant dice pool when that skill is used, unless explicitly stated otherwise.
entries.raw: - The level of a T1 Skill is always added to the relevant dice pool when that skill is used, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

entries.text: Special effects granted by T1 Skills are applied after dice pool construction and penalties, and before result comparison, unless explicitly stated otherwise.
entries.raw: - Special effects granted by T1 Skills are applied after dice pool construction and penalties, and before result comparison, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

entries.text: "Lowered DC (-1)" means the required success threshold for the roll is reduced by 1.
entries.raw: - "Lowered DC (-1)" means the required success threshold for the roll is reduced by 1.

entries.text: Social is used for both offensive and defensive interpersonal rolls unless explicitly overridden.
entries.raw: - Social is used for both offensive and defensive interpersonal rolls unless explicitly overridden.

entries.text: Level 1: Enables unarmed and melee weapon attacks. Attack roll: DEX + Melee.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Enables unarmed and melee weapon attacks. Attack roll: DEX + Melee.

entries.text: Level 5: Rolls of 10 count as 2 successes on melee attack and damage rolls.
entries.raw: - Level 5: Rolls of 10 count as 2 successes on melee attack and damage rolls.

entries.text: Level 1: Enables ranged weapon attacks. Attack roll: DEX + Ranged.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Enables ranged weapon attacks. Attack roll: DEX + Ranged.

entries.text: Level 5: Rolls of 10 count as 2 successes on ranged attack and damage rolls.
entries.raw: - Level 5: Rolls of 10 count as 2 successes on ranged attack and damage rolls.

entries.text: Level 1: Governs running, acrobatics, jumping, and flexibility.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Governs running, acrobatics, jumping, and flexibility.

entries.text: Level 3: Gain bonus Armor Class (AC). Bonus AC = Athletics Level - 2.
entries.raw: - Level 3: Gain bonus Armor Class (AC). Bonus AC = Athletics Level - 2.

entries.text: Level 4: Base running speed increases from 25 m/turn to 30 m/turn.
entries.raw: - Level 4: Base running speed increases from 25 m/turn to 30 m/turn.

entries.text: Level 5: Athletics rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).
entries.raw: - Level 5: Athletics rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).

entries.text: Level 1: Enter Stealth outside combat. Roll: DEX + Stealth.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Enter Stealth outside combat. Roll: DEX + Stealth.

entries.text: Level 2: The first melee or ranged attack made from Stealth removes the target's Dodge for that attack.
entries.raw: - Level 2: The first melee or ranged attack made from Stealth removes the target's Dodge for that attack.

entries.text: Level 3: Ignore penalties and target-based resistance modifiers applied specifically against Stealth rolls.
entries.raw: - Level 3: Ignore penalties and target-based resistance modifiers applied specifically against Stealth rolls.

entries.text: Level 4: The first magical attack made from Stealth removes the target's Stamina soak for that attack.
entries.raw: - Level 4: The first magical attack made from Stealth removes the target's Stamina soak for that attack.

entries.text: Level 5: Stealth rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).
entries.raw: - Level 5: Stealth rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).

entries.text: Level 1: Detect hidden creatures and traps. Roll: PER + Alertness.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Detect hidden creatures and traps. Roll: PER + Alertness.

entries.text: Level 2: Disarm traps and pick locks. Roll: DEX + Alertness.
entries.raw: - Level 2: Disarm traps and pick locks. Roll: DEX + Alertness.

entries.text: Level 3: Add Awareness supernatural power level to Alertness rolls, if applicable.
entries.raw: - Level 3: Add Awareness supernatural power level to Alertness rolls, if applicable.

entries.text: Level 4: May use INT + Alertness to analyze or identify instead of detecting.
entries.raw: - Level 4: May use INT + Alertness to analyze or identify instead of detecting.

entries.text: Level 5: Alertness rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).
entries.raw: - Level 5: Alertness rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).

entries.text: Level 1: Enables social influence rolls:
entries.raw: - Level 1: Enables social influence rolls:

entries.text: Level 2: Gain +1 die on all Social rolls.
entries.raw: - Level 2: Gain +1 die on all Social rolls.

entries.text: Level 3: On a successful Social roll, gain 1 Temporary Inspiration. Temporary Inspiration may be spent to reroll a failed roll. Maximum 3 Temporary Inspiration at any time.
entries.raw: - Level 3: On a successful Social roll, gain 1 Temporary Inspiration. Temporary Inspiration may be spent to reroll a failed roll. Maximum 3 Temporary Inspiration at any time.

entries.text: Level 4: Once per scene, reroll one failed Social roll and keep the better result.
entries.raw: - Level 4: Once per scene, reroll one failed Social roll and keep the better result.

entries.text: Level 5: Social rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).
entries.raw: - Level 5: Social rolls are easier. Apply Lowered DC (-1).

entries.text: Level 1: Stabilize bleeding and heal 1 HP. Usable once per day per target. Not usable in combat. Takes 2 minutes.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Stabilize bleeding and heal 1 HP. Usable once per day per target. Not usable in combat. Takes 2 minutes.

entries.text: Level 2: Usable twice per day per target.
entries.raw: - Level 2: Usable twice per day per target.

entries.text: Level 3: Heal 2 HP. Cure weak poisons when first aid is applied.
entries.raw: - Level 3: Heal 2 HP. Cure weak poisons when first aid is applied.

entries.text: Level 4: Perform surgery if conditions are met. Cure weak diseases.
entries.raw: - Level 4: Perform surgery if conditions are met. Cure weak diseases.

entries.text: Level 5: Heal 3 HP. Cure moderate poisons and stat-penalty conditions. Targets at 0 to -5 HP are restored to 2 HP.
entries.raw: - Level 5: Heal 3 HP. Cure moderate poisons and stat-penalty conditions. Targets at 0 to -5 HP are restored to 2 HP.

entries.text: Level 1: Effectively use computers, mobile devices, and common high-tech tools.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Effectively use computers, mobile devices, and common high-tech tools.

entries.text: Level 2: Identify and understand unknown high-tech devices based on roll success. May study portal-related items and enhance item qualities with DM approval. Gain 1 Tech Point (TP).
entries.raw: - Level 2: Identify and understand unknown high-tech devices based on roll success. May study portal-related items and enhance item qualities with DM approval. Gain 1 Tech Point (TP).

entries.text: Level 3: Hack non-secure systems. Roll: INT + Technology vs INT + Technology (developer). Gain 2 TP total.
entries.raw: - Level 3: Hack non-secure systems. Roll: INT + Technology vs INT + Technology (developer). Gain 2 TP total.

entries.text: Level 4: Hack secure systems and mobile devices. Roll: INT + Technology vs INT + Technology (developer) + firewall rank. May act as network administrator in limited organizations. Gain 3 TP total.
entries.raw: - Level 4: Hack secure systems and mobile devices. Roll: INT + Technology vs INT + Technology (developer) + firewall rank. May act as network administrator in limited organizations. Gain 3 TP total.

entries.text: Level 5: Attempt hacks against systems protected by specialist teams. Roll: INT + Technology (developer) + firewall rank + specialist bonus. May act as network administrator in most organizations. Gain 4 TP total.
entries.raw: - Level 5: Attempt hacks against systems protected by specialist teams. Roll: INT + Technology (developer) + firewall rank + specialist bonus. May act as network administrator in most organizations. Gain 4 TP total.

entries.text: Spend 1 TP to increase an item's numeric property by +25%, rounded up.
entries.raw: - Spend 1 TP to increase an item's numeric property by +25%, rounded up.

entries.text: Any numeric property may be enhanced, subject to DM discretion.
entries.raw: - Any numeric property may be enhanced, subject to DM discretion.

entries.text: Maximum 2 TP may be assigned to a single item.
entries.raw: - Maximum 2 TP may be assigned to a single item.

entries.text: TP are reusable and reassigned:
entries.raw: - TP are reusable and reassigned:

children.text: Assignments remain active until the next long rest.
children.raw: - Assignments remain active until the next long rest.

children.text: Before that next long rest, TP may be reassigned.
children.raw: - Before that next long rest, TP may be reassigned.

entries.text: Level 1: Graduate-level knowledge in one chosen natural science. Eligible for entry-level white-collar roles.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Graduate-level knowledge in one chosen natural science. Eligible for entry-level white-collar roles.

entries.text: Level 2: Master's-level expertise. May conduct advanced research. May study portal-related supernatural phenomena and enhance supernatural powers with DM approval. Gain 1 Enhancement Point (EP).
entries.raw: - Level 2: Master's-level expertise. May conduct advanced research. May study portal-related supernatural phenomena and enhance supernatural powers with DM approval. Gain 1 Enhancement Point (EP).

entries.text: Level 3: PhD or MD in one field. Eligible for senior academic or professional roles. Gain 2 EP total.
entries.raw: - Level 3: PhD or MD in one field. Eligible for senior academic or professional roles. Gain 2 EP total.

entries.text: Level 4: Professor-level authority. May teach and access university resources. Minimum INT 3. Gain 3 EP total.
entries.raw: - Level 4: Professor-level authority. May teach and access university resources. Minimum INT 3. Gain 3 EP total.

entries.text: Level 5: World-class authority in the field. Near-global academic access. Minimum INT 4. Gain 4 EP total.
entries.raw: - Level 5: World-class authority in the field. Near-global academic access. Minimum INT 4. Gain 4 EP total.

entries.text: Spend 1 EP to increase a supernatural power's numeric effect by +25%, rounded up.
entries.raw: - Spend 1 EP to increase a supernatural power's numeric effect by +25%, rounded up.

entries.text: Any numeric effect may be enhanced, subject to DM discretion.
entries.raw: - Any numeric effect may be enhanced, subject to DM discretion.

entries.text: Maximum 2 EP may be assigned to a single power.
entries.raw: - Maximum 2 EP may be assigned to a single power.

entries.text: EP are reusable and reassigned:
entries.raw: - EP are reusable and reassigned:

children.text: Assignments remain active until the next long rest.
children.raw: - Assignments remain active until the next long rest.

children.text: Before that next long rest, EP may be reassigned.
children.raw: - Before that next long rest, EP may be reassigned.

entries.text: Level 1: Repair common, masterwork, and uncommon items with tools. Ride bicycles. Typical duration: 10 minutes-8 hours.
entries.raw: - Level 1: Repair common, masterwork, and uncommon items with tools. Ride bicycles. Typical duration: 10 minutes-8 hours.

entries.text: Level 2: Repair rare items. Drive cars, buses, and motorbikes. Begin crafting common items.
entries.raw: - Level 2: Repair rare items. Drive cars, buses, and motorbikes. Begin crafting common items.

entries.text: Level 3: Craft uncommon items. Repair epic items. Drive all land vehicles. Modify uncommon and rare items using DEX + Mechanics.
entries.raw: - Level 3: Craft uncommon items. Repair epic items. Drive all land vehicles. Modify uncommon and rare items using DEX + Mechanics.

entries.text: Level 4: Craft masterwork and uncommon weapons, armor, and shields. Cost: 1/5 item value. Operate aircraft and ships. Modify epic items.
entries.raw: - Level 4: Craft masterwork and uncommon weapons, armor, and shields. Cost: 1/5 item value. Operate aircraft and ships. Modify epic items.

entries.text: Level 5: Repair and modify legendary items. On great success, modifications may exceed item quality. Add INT dice to modification rolls.
entries.raw: - Level 5: Repair and modify legendary items. On great success, modifications may exceed item quality. Add INT dice to modification rolls.

entries.text: Level 1: Know names and basic properties of most T1 supernatural powers. May learn 1 T1 power. Gain Mana Bonus = ceil(XP Used / 100).
entries.raw: - Level 1: Know names and basic properties of most T1 supernatural powers. May learn 1 T1 power. Gain Mana Bonus = ceil(XP Used / 100).

entries.text: Level 2: May learn 2 T1 supernatural powers.
entries.raw: - Level 2: May learn 2 T1 supernatural powers.

entries.text: Level 3: May learn 3 T1 supernatural powers. Gain Mana Bonus = ceil(XP Used / 50).
entries.raw: - Level 3: May learn 3 T1 supernatural powers. Gain Mana Bonus = ceil(XP Used / 50).

entries.text: Level 4: May learn 1 T2 supernatural power.
entries.raw: - Level 4: May learn 1 T2 supernatural power.

entries.text: Level 5: May learn 2 T2 supernatural powers. Gain Mana Bonus = ceil(XP Used / 25).
entries.raw: - Level 5: May learn 2 T2 supernatural powers. Gain Mana Bonus = ceil(XP Used / 25).

entries.text: This section defines meta-level systems that modify or contextualize core mechanics, including reroll resources, world-impact tracking, and character-level advantages and disadvantages.
entries.raw: - This section defines meta-level systems that modify or contextualize core mechanics, including reroll resources, world-impact tracking, and character-level advantages and disadvantages.

entries.text: Inspiration represents willpower-based rerolls.
entries.raw: Inspiration represents willpower-based rerolls.

entries.text: Non-temporary Inspiration
entries.raw: - Non-temporary Inspiration

entries.text: Temporary Inspiration
entries.raw: - Temporary Inspiration

entries.text: Either type may be spent to reroll any failed roll at any time.
entries.raw: Either type may be spent to reroll any failed roll at any time.

entries.text: Awarded by the DM for:
entries.raw: - Awarded by the DM for:

entries.text: Non-temporary Inspiration points stack.
entries.raw: - Non-temporary Inspiration points stack.

entries.text: Granted by specific rules or perks (e.g., the Inspired Merit).
entries.raw: - Granted by specific rules or perks (e.g., the Inspired Merit).

entries.text: Temporary Inspiration points stack only if granted from different sources.
entries.raw: - Temporary Inspiration points stack only if granted from different sources.

entries.text: Temporary Inspiration points expire at the end of the game session.
entries.raw: - Temporary Inspiration points expire at the end of the game session.

entries.text: Karma represents the measurable impact of an entity's actions on the world.
entries.raw: - Karma represents the measurable impact of an entity's actions on the world.

entries.text: Karma is assigned and adjusted by the DM based on observed impact, not intent.
entries.raw: - Karma is assigned and adjusted by the DM based on observed impact, not intent.

entries.text: Karma is not an alignment system and does not measure morality.
entries.raw: - Karma is not an alignment system and does not measure morality.

entries.text: Both constructive and destructive impacts are tracked.
entries.raw: - Both constructive and destructive impacts are tracked.

entries.text: An entity may hold both positive and negative Karma over time.
entries.raw: - An entity may hold both positive and negative Karma over time.

entries.text: Karma is a numeric value in the range [-24, +24].
entries.raw: - Karma is a numeric value in the range [-24, +24].

entries.text: -24: Prime destructive entity; maximum world-scale harm
entries.raw: - -24: Prime destructive entity; maximum world-scale harm

entries.text: -6 to -12: Global-scale mass suffering
entries.raw: - -6 to -12: Global-scale mass suffering

entries.text: -4 to -8: National-scale harm
entries.raw: - -4 to -8: National-scale harm

entries.text: -2 to -6: Localized severe harm
entries.raw: - -2 to -6: Localized severe harm

entries.text: 0: Negligible or insignificant impact
entries.raw: - 0: Negligible or insignificant impact

entries.text: +2 to +6: Meaningful positive influence
entries.raw: - +2 to +6: Meaningful positive influence

entries.text: +4 to +8: Major positive influence on communities or nations
entries.raw: - +4 to +8: Major positive influence on communities or nations

entries.text: +12: World-level beneficial influence
entries.raw: - +12: World-level beneficial influence

entries.text: +24: Prime constructive entity; maximum world-scale healing and harmony
entries.raw: - +24: Prime constructive entity; maximum world-scale healing and harmony

entries.text: Merits cost XP and apply immediately upon acquisition.
entries.raw: Merits cost XP and apply immediately upon acquisition.

entries.text: Gain +1 base HP. May be taken up to 2 times.
entries.raw: Gain +1 base HP. May be taken up to 2 times.

entries.text: XP Cost: 5 / 10
entries.raw: XP Cost: 5 / 10
entries.label: xp_cost

entries.text: Gain +1 base Mana. May be taken up to 4 times.
entries.raw: Gain +1 base Mana. May be taken up to 4 times.

entries.text: XP Cost: 3 / 6 / 9 / 12
entries.raw: XP Cost: 3 / 6 / 9 / 12
entries.label: xp_cost

entries.text: Stat soft cap increases to 6 instead of 5.
entries.raw: Stat soft cap increases to 6 instead of 5.

entries.text: Restriction: Cannot be taken with Weak and Meek.
entries.raw: Restriction: Cannot be taken with Weak and Meek.
entries.label: restriction
entries.value: Cannot be taken with Weak and Meek.

entries.text: XP Cost: 20
entries.raw: XP Cost: 20
entries.label: xp_cost

entries.text: Start with a high standard of living (home, car, $500,000 funds, $5,000/month passive income). Trivial purchases are ignored.
entries.raw: Start with a high standard of living (home, car, $500,000 funds, $5,000/month passive income). Trivial purchases are ignored.

entries.text: Restriction: Cannot be taken with Huge Debt.
entries.raw: Restriction: Cannot be taken with Huge Debt.
entries.label: restriction
entries.value: Cannot be taken with Huge Debt.

entries.text: XP Cost: 5
entries.raw: XP Cost: 5
entries.label: xp_cost

entries.text: Gain a close companion (friend or lover). Companions are non-hunters with low combat capability but may provide situational assistance. May be taken up to 2 times.
entries.raw: Gain a close companion (friend or lover). Companions are non-hunters with low combat capability but may provide situational assistance. May be taken up to 2 times.

entries.text: XP Cost: 5 / 10
entries.raw: XP Cost: 5 / 10
entries.label: xp_cost

entries.text: Learn one additional language. May be taken up to 2 x starting INT.
entries.raw: Learn one additional language. May be taken up to 2 x starting INT.

entries.text: XP Cost: 2 per language at character creation.
entries.raw: XP Cost: 2 per language at character creation.
entries.label: xp_cost

entries.text: Note: Languages learned later cost 4 XP and require 5 sessions to master.
entries.raw: Note: Languages learned later cost 4 XP and require 5 sessions to master.
entries.label: note
entries.value: Languages learned later cost 4 XP and require 5 sessions to master.

entries.text: Choose Pitta, Vata, or Kapha. Gain resistance (half damage) to associated elements.
entries.raw: Choose Pitta, Vata, or Kapha. Gain resistance (half damage) to associated elements.

entries.text: Pitta: fire, acid
entries.raw: - Pitta: fire, acid

entries.text: Vata: lightning, cold
entries.raw: - Vata: lightning, cold

entries.text: Kapha: cold, acid
entries.raw: - Kapha: cold, acid

entries.text: Resistance Formula: `ceil((Total Damage / 2) - Stamina)`
entries.raw: Resistance Formula: `ceil((Total Damage / 2) - Stamina)`
entries.label: resistance_formula

entries.raw: XP Cost: 15

entries.text: Start with 5 non-temporary Inspiration points.
entries.raw: - Start with 5 non-temporary Inspiration points.

entries.text: Gain 1 temporary Inspiration at the start of each session.
entries.raw: - Gain 1 temporary Inspiration at the start of each session.

entries.text: Temporary Inspiration expires at session end.
entries.raw: - Temporary Inspiration expires at session end.

entries.text: XP Cost: 5
entries.raw: XP Cost: 5

entries.raw: Flaws grant XP and apply immediately upon acquisition.

entries.text: Reduce base HP by 1. May be taken up to 2 times.
entries.raw: Reduce base HP by 1. May be taken up to 2 times.

entries.raw: May learn only 1 T1 supernatural power (and later 1 T2, if available), regardless of Occult level.

entries.raw: - The character cannot spend non-temporary Inspiration.

entries.text: Each time the character would gain non-temporary Inspiration, reduce this flaw's potency by 1 instead.
entries.raw: - Each time the character would gain non-temporary Inspiration, reduce this flaw's potency by 1 instead.

entries.text: After 10 such reductions, this flaw is removed and Inspiration functions normally.

entries.text: This section is descriptive only.
entries.raw: - This section is descriptive only.

entries.text: It provides narrative context for Rank tiers.
entries.raw: - It provides narrative context for Rank tiers.

entries.text: It has no mechanical effect unless explicitly referenced elsewhere.
entries.raw: - It has no mechanical effect unless explicitly referenced elsewhere.

entries.text: Rank F: Ordinary humans with negligible world impact.
entries.raw: - Rank F: Ordinary humans with negligible world impact.

entries.text: Rank E: Exceptional individuals or entry-level hunters.
entries.raw: - Rank E: Exceptional individuals or entry-level hunters.

entries.text: Rank D: Clearly superhuman entities.
entries.raw: - Rank D: Clearly superhuman entities.

entries.text: Rank C: Superhero-tier beings; elite hunters and major threats.
entries.raw: - Rank C: Superhero-tier beings; elite hunters and major threats.

entries.text: Rank B: Mythic or demi-god-level entities.
entries.raw: - Rank B: Mythic or demi-god-level entities.

entries.text: Rank A: World-shaping forces beyond common myth.
entries.raw: - Rank A: World-shaping forces beyond common myth.

entries.text: Rank S: Singular anomalies; narrative outliers.
entries.raw: - Rank S: Singular anomalies; narrative outliers.
